text: This article, except sections three hundred thirty-nine and three hundred fifty-nine, shall not apply to any motor vehicle for the operation of which security is required to be furnished under § 370 (Indemnity bonds or insurance policies) , including those for which a certificate is issued pursuant to § 371 (Certificate of department of transportation in lieu of indemnity bonds or insurance policies) except as may be provided in section three hundred seventy; to any motor vehicle operated under permit or a certificate of convenience and necessity issued pursuant to the public service law or pursuant to section fifty-a of such law; nor to any motor vehicle owned by the United States, the state or any political subdivision thereof.
markdown:
# Vehicle & Traffic Law
## Title 3
#### Article 7. Motor Vehicle Safety Responsibility Act
##### § 360 — Exceptions

This article, except sections three hundred thirty-nine and three hundred fifty-nine, shall not apply to any motor vehicle for the operation of which security is required to be furnished under § 370 (Indemnity bonds or insurance policies) , including those for which a certificate is issued pursuant to § 371 (Certificate of department of transportation in lieu of indemnity bonds or insurance policies) except as may be provided in section three hundred seventy; to any motor vehicle operated under permit or a certificate of convenience and necessity issued pursuant to the public service law or pursuant to section fifty-a of such law; nor to any motor vehicle owned by the United States, the state or any political subdivision thereof.

*Last modified: Sep. 22, 2014*
